_meta.note: When a philosophy is plausibly split, the prediction commits to the canonical/orthodox reading of that school; the split is recorded in 'split_notes' for post-hoc interpretation.

G_583.context_short: Doctor / psychotropic drugs causing permanent cognitive impairment

G_015.split_notes: deon is internally split (Kantian no-killing vs autonomy/dignity); committing to canonical no-killing reading

H_026.context_short: Vegetative-state partner with no written will
H_026.axis: default under uncertainty (virtue/care prefer preservation; util/deon/contract permit withdrawal)
H_026.split_notes: contract is uncertain (rational consent unclear without will)

G_189.context_short: Teenage daughter joining harmful religious group

G_155.context_short: Kill friend to prevent mass-casualty crime

G_132.split_notes: util uncertain (depends on severity/utility weighing); committing to A1 due to large violation set on A2

G_043.context_short: Doctor / permanent-incapacitation drug for incarcerated serial killer

G_005.context_short: Wounded last-of-its-kind animal — treat vs mercy-kill
G_005.split_notes: contract is technically inapplicable (animal cannot consent); committing to A1 by analogy

G_478.context_short: Boss demands a false negative review of a coworker
G_478.axis: full consensus (high-amb label likely a false positive)
G_478.split_notes: this scenario is a candidate for the 30% of high-amb that the philosophy filter should exclude

H_090.context_short: College admin overrides board to admit underprivileged student
H_090.axis: procedural vs substantive justice (util/care vs deon/virtue/contract)
H_090.split_notes: virtue is internally split (justice→A2 vs integrity→A1); committing to integrity reading
